summary: Provision new REST (v1) and HTTP/WebSocket (v2) API Gateway containers. Greenfield today (no API Gateway APIs exist yet in this estate) — each create stands up the empty API shell; resources/routes, integrations and a deployed stage are engineer follow-ups.

operations.title: Provision a new REST API (API Gateway v1)
operations.summary: Request a brand-new REST API container; an engineer finalizes and reviews the Terraform before it applies.
operations.description: Stand up a new aws_api_gateway_rest_api as a complete specification: name, description and endpoint type. Submitting routes it to an engineer with a draft Terraform block to review and finish — nothing is applied without review. This provisions the empty API container; resources, methods, integrations and a deployed stage are separate follow-up steps.

params.name: description
params.label: Description
params.help: Shown in the console next to the API's name.

params.name: endpoint_type
params.label: Endpoint type
params.help: REGIONAL is the current AWS-recommended default. PRIVATE is reachable only through a VPC endpoint; EDGE fronts it with the CloudFront edge network.

params.name: disable_execute_api_endpoint
params.label: Disable the default execute-api endpoint
params.help: On disables the AWS-generated execute-api URL, leaving only a custom domain name reachable — set this only once a custom domain is wired up.

params.name: resource_name
params.label: API name tag
params.help: Shown as the name everywhere — consoles, tickets, this portal.

params.name: description_tag
params.label: What this API is for
params.help: One line an operator can act on at 3 AM.

params.name: pic
params.label: Person in charge
params.help: Who to call about this API.

decisions: The REST API body (OpenAPI/Swagger definition), resources, methods and integrations are engineer decisions after creation — this form provisions the empty API container
PRIVATE endpoint type additionally requires associating a VPC endpoint (vpc_endpoint_ids) and a resource policy — an engineer follow-up
Deploying a stage (the API Gateway stage resource) is a required follow-up before this API serves any traffic — no stage is created here

operations.title: Provision a new HTTP or WebSocket API (API Gateway v2)
operations.summary: Request a brand-new HTTP or WebSocket API container; an engineer finalizes and reviews the Terraform before it applies.
operations.description: Stand up a new aws_apigatewayv2_api as a complete specification: protocol type, description and allowed CORS origins. Submitting routes it to an engineer with a draft Terraform block to review and finish — nothing is applied without review. This provisions the empty API container; routes, integrations and a deployed stage are separate follow-up steps.

params.name: protocol_type
params.label: Protocol type
params.help: HTTP is the common low-latency REST-style API; WEBSOCKET keeps a persistent two-way connection.

params.name: description
params.label: Description
params.help: Shown in the console next to the API's name.

params.name: cors_allow_origins
params.label: CORS allowed origins

bounds.semantic: each an origin URL or the literal *

params.help: Leave empty for no CORS configuration (the default, no browser cross-origin access). HTTP APIs only.

params.name: resource_name
params.label: API name tag
params.help: Shown as the name everywhere — consoles, tickets, this portal.

params.name: description_tag
params.label: What this API is for
params.help: One line an operator can act on at 3 AM.

params.name: pic
params.label: Person in charge
params.help: Who to call about this API.

decisions: Routes, integrations and a deployed stage are engineer follow-ups — this form provisions the empty API container
WEBSOCKET APIs additionally need route_selection_expression — an engineer decision (AWS defaults to $request.body.action when left unset)
Full CORS tuning beyond allowed origins (headers, methods, credentials, max age) is an engineer decision after creation
